title: PostgreSQL Integration, Loopback HTTP, Fault Injection, and Reconciliation · Language Lens: Feed Go Database Faults
summary: Mocks confirm SQL strings but not constraints, HTTP tests skip redirects and compression, retries never meet real transaction errors, and cleanup is not asserted. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go database faults increment in disposable end-to-end ingestion harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Inject constraint, serialization, deadlock, lock wait, pool exhaustion, cancellation, connection loss, and commit ambiguity with expected recovery.
Use httptest origins and custom transports for validators, redirects, DNS decisions, compression, slow bodies, malformed bytes, disconnects, and publisher budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
